title: Unvalidated Input – SQL Injection Vulnerability
description: The system does not adequately sanitize or validate user input before using it in database queries. This could allow an attacker to inject malicious SQL code, potentially compromising the entire database.
attack_vector: An attacker injects malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes) that bypasses input validation and is executed by the database.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements.
Use an Object-Relational Mapper (ORM) that handles input sanitization.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.
